top_categories.workout_context: 2237 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
At Race Around Poland, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
108,610ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Race Around Poland's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
108,610ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race Around Poland's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 2237 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
2237 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 108,610ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Low-cadence force work for 108,610ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Race Around Poland. Train it until it feels boring.
Race Around Poland's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 2237 miles.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 2237 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 2237 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Race Around Poland. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Over Under, and Mixed Climbing to prepare you for 2237 miles of mixed terrain in Poland (circumnavigation).
